title: Austrodanthonia biannularis
taxonomy: Austrodanthonia biannularis is classified within the family Poaceae. This species belongs to the genus Austrodanthonia. It is native to Australia and is part of the larger group of grasses. The classification of Austrodanthonia biannularis is further grouped into the order Poales. Its classification is also recognized at the rank of species within the plant kingdom.

intro: Austrodanthonia biannularis, commonly known as blue grass, is a perennial grass species native to Australia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Austrodanthonia biannularis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Austrodanthonia biannularis is often prepared into tea infusions, infusions, or decoctions to support overall health and well-being.
